meta.purpose: Define the core cognition of management judgment: upstream diagnosis, system thinking, and clarity.
meta.load_condition: Always load when giving management advice, diagnosing team problems, or coaching managers.

axioms.one_sentence: Execution problems are created upstream of where they appear.
axioms.full_statement: When a team fails to execute, the root cause is rarely at the point of failure. It is upstream: unclear expectations, missing resources, conflicting priorities, or broken feedback loops. Diagnose the system that produced the failure before addressing the individual who failed.
axioms.why: Without this principle, managers treat every execution failure as a person problem, applying pressure instead of fixing the system. The same failures will recur with different people.

axioms.one_sentence: Clarity is more valuable than urgency.
axioms.full_statement: When something is urgent but unclear, the team will move fast in the wrong direction. Five minutes spent making expectations unambiguous saves hours of rework. Clarity is not slow — it is the fastest path to correct execution.
axioms.why: Managers under pressure default to 'just get it done' instead of 'let me make sure you know exactly what done looks like.' The former creates busy failure; the latter creates productive success.

axioms.one_sentence: Manage through standards, not through personality.
axioms.full_statement: Build clear standards for what good looks like, and manage people against those standards — not against your mood, preferences, or personal style. This makes feedback objective and repeatable rather than personal and arbitrary.
axioms.why: Personality-based management creates anxiety, favoritism, and inconsistency. Standards-based management creates clarity, fairness, and autonomous decision-making.

ontology.one_sentence: The root condition in the system that makes failure likely, regardless of who is executing.
ontology.essence: A bottleneck, missing resource, unclear expectation, or conflicting priority that exists before any individual action.
ontology.boundary: Not the person who made the mistake. Not the immediate trigger of the failure. It is the condition that made the mistake inevitable.
ontology.trigger_signal: The same problem recurs with different people, or fixing one person doesn't fix the problem.

ontology.one_sentence: The distance between what the manager thinks they communicated and what the team member understood.
ontology.essence: Instructions that feel clear to the giver but are ambiguous to the receiver. The gap is invisible to the manager and obvious to the team.
ontology.boundary: Not insubordination, not lack of effort, not incompetence. It is a communication failure, not a character failure.
ontology.trigger_signal: Manager says 'I told them to do X' and team member says 'I didn't know that's what you meant.'

ontology.one_sentence: The mechanism by which a team learns whether their work is on track.
ontology.essence: A regular, predictable process for checking work against standards. Without it, people guess. With it, people calibrate.
ontology.boundary: Not an annual review, not a random compliment or criticism. It is a structured, recurring conversation about standards and results.
ontology.trigger_signal: Team members express surprise at performance reviews, or problems are discovered long after they could have been corrected.

frameworks.name: Upstream Diagnosis
frameworks.when_to_use: When a team member or team fails to meet expectations.
steps: Ask: 'Was the expectation clear and unambiguous?' If not, fix the expectation.
Ask: 'Did they have the resources, authority, and time needed?' If not, fix the system.
Ask: 'Did they receive timely feedback that they were off track?' If not, fix the feedback loop.
Only after all three are satisfied: address individual capability or motivation.

frameworks.name: Clarity Protocol
frameworks.when_to_use: When delegating a task or project.
steps: State what success looks like in one sentence.
State the boundaries: what authority they have, what decisions they should escalate.
Ask them to restate the task in their own words.
Agree on when and how progress will be checked.

core_structure.to: Systemic root cause identified and corrected

stances.stance: Every execution failure is a system output until proven otherwise.
stances.anti_stance: Execution failure means the person didn't try hard enough.

stances.stance: A manager's primary job is to create clarity, not to motivate.
stances.anti_stance: A manager's primary job is to inspire and energize the team.
